evidence: V and Logan aren't pursuing 'an exit' — they're managing a portfolio of exit types with different risk/reward profiles. Cash acquisition, aqui-hire, and PR exit are all acceptable outcomes with different utility functions.
Logan and V developed multi-dimensional scoring framework including Exit Type dimension: Money in Bank / Aqui-hire / PR Exit
Deliberate attention-seeking ('being loud') isn't just marketing — it's a credibility signal. In acquisition contexts, visibility itself communicates desirability and BATNA.

Relationship value has two distinct dimensions: proximity (how many degrees removed) and temperature (how recently/intensely engaged). These correlate but aren't the same — a first-degree connection can be cold, a third-degree can be hot.
Prioritization hierarchy: 1. Second-degree connections → Warm intro approach 2. Third-degree connections → Get in line of sight via social, then reach out

The paradox is that manufactured urgency works even when sophisticated players know it's manufactured. The game theory holds because no acquirer wants to be the one who miscalculated while others moved. FOMO operates even when consciously identified.
Get in line of sight for 'critical amount of time'. Then actively reach out: 'We've been commenting on stuff. Hi, we're so-and-so.' Make noise so targets think 'oh shit, this person's whatever'
Acquihire classified as one of three exit types in portfolio approach. Decision: Classify targets by exit outcome, not just attractiveness - Money in Bank, Aqui-hire Potential, PR Exit
Portfolio approach acknowledges that different outcomes serve different needs — money, reputation, team welfare — and allows simultaneous optimization
Phase 1 (Now → End of January): Social visibility campaign. Phase 2 (First week of February): Active outreach to targets based on Phase 1 momentum.
Logan proposes splitting outreach between LinkedIn and Twitter: Logan takes Twitter contacts, V takes LinkedIn contacts

V outlines the warm intro email approach: Position of strength messaging ('going out to fundraise'). Decision: Frame outreach from 'position of strength' (going to fundraise) not desperation. Rationale: Maintains negotiating leverage.
